youtube_items.title: [YT] Progress 95 Cargo Ship Docking
youtube_items.summary: Good evening, from Michigan, in Houston, in the International Space Station flight control room here at NASA's Johnson Space Center. Here's a look at the orbit 3 team, led by flight director Diane Daley and her team of flight controllers working in Tando at the Russian Mission Control Center on the outskirts of Moscow. Teams are monitoring the automated arrival of the Uncrewed Progress 95 spacecraft. It'll talk to the airport of the Thursday service module here in about 45 minutes. This is the final leg of its two-day 33 orbit journey, carrying nearly three tons of food fuel and supplies to the exhibition for crew of seven living and working on the International Space Station. Here you see on your screen just activated at the time we began coverage with a view from the external television

analysis.problem: SpaceX launches multiple Earth observation satellites on Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy rockets, which may contribute to increased satellite debris in low Earth orbit.
analysis.root_cause: The repeated launches of satellites, including Earth observation satellites, increase the number of objects in low Earth orbit, potentially leading to orbital debris.
leverage_points: SpaceX as the primary launcher of Earth observation satellites, contributing to orbital debris.
The frequency of launches on Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy rockets, which are used for multiple payloads.

proposed_actions.action: Implement satellite debris tracking and mitigation protocols for all launches.
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce the number of debris objects in low Earth orbit by 30% within 12 months.

evidence: SpaceX launches 24 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rockets from Vandenberg SFB in 2026.
The CAS500-2 mission launched a South Korean Earth observation satellite on a Falcon 9 rocket.
analysis.generalization: This problem is related to the increasing number of satellites in low Earth orbit, which impacts space operations and sustainability.
analysis.summary: SpaceX's frequent launches of Earth observation satellites contribute to orbital debris, requiring mitigation strategies to reduce debris accumulation.

raw_items.title: Taylor Geospatial unveils global field dataset
raw_items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO – The first global dataset showing the boundaries of agricultural fields was released in late April, after an 18-month campaign by geospatial experts in industry and academia

raw_items.title: SpaceX launches South Korean Earth observation satellite, plus 44 more payloads on midnight Falcon 9 rideshare mission
raw_items.summary: The CAS500-2 mission lifted off from pad 4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base at 12 a.m. PDT (3 a.m. EDT / 0700 UTC). The Korea Aerospace Industries’ Compact Advanced Satellite 500 (CAS500) satellite was the first to deploy about an hour after liftoff. <a class="mh-excerpt-more" href="https://spaceflightnow.com/2026/05/02/live-coverage-spacex-to-launch-south-korean-earth-observation-satellite-44-more-payloads-on-overnight-falcon-9-rideshare-mission/" title="SpaceX launches South Korean Earth observ

raw_items.title: SpaceX marks May Day, National Space Day with Starlink mission on a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral
raw_items.summary: The Starlink 10-38 mission saw SpaceX deploy 29 broadband internet satellites into low Earth orbit. Liftoff from pad 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station happened at 2:06:10 p.m. EDT (1806:10 UTC).

raw_items.title: SpaceX launches 24 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg SFB
raw_items.summary: The Starlink 17-36 mission was SpaceX’s 51st Falcon 9 launch of 2026. Liftoff from pad 4E happened at 7:42:49 pm PDT (10:42:49 pm EDT / 0242:49 UTC).

raw_items.title: SpaceX launches 6-ton ViaSat-3 F3 satellite on Falcon Heavy rocket
raw_items.summary: The mission was the 12th launch of a Falcon Heavy rocket to date. The rocket lifted off from pad 39A at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center at 10:13 a.m. EDT (1413 UTC) on Wednesday, April 29.
